$comment: Boundary-case fixture for the distributed-boundary matrix. Not a live release packet. See docs/compat/boundary_matrix.md.
scenario: Launcher was promoted to launcher_contract@2 while an indexer helper from the prior coordinated artifact set (launcher_contract@1) remained on disk. Coordinated_artifact_set_only boundaries cannot continue under a mixed set. The launcher refuses to start with a visible fail-closed reason and offers a rollback to the prior coordinated set.

supported_skew_window.summary: Launcher and helper binaries come from one coordinated artifact set with shared build identity, contract version, and schema epoch.

upgrade_order.notes: Promote the full launcher-plus-helpers artifact graph together; mixed startup outside the coordinated set is refused, not warned.

rollback_order.notes: Rollback restores the prior coordinated set, never a partial mix.

unsupported_state_behavior.contract_rule: Refuse mixed launcher/helper startup with a visible reason; offer rollback to the prior coordinated artifact set.

what_happens_when_upgrade_order_violated: Startup refuses. The fail-closed visible reason names the coordinated artifact set, the detected mixed helper, and the rollback repair path. No partial startup, no warning-only mode. The worked envelope is fixtures/compat/mixed_version_cases/upgrade_order_violation.json.
what_happens_when_skew_window_exceeded: Equivalent to upgrade-order violation on this boundary: any mix outside the coordinated set is out of window and refuses startup. There is no degrade path; coordinated-artifact-set-only boundaries are not runnable under a mixed set.
visible_fail_closed_reason_example: Aureline detected a mixed launcher and helper set. The launcher was updated but at least one helper binary is from the prior coordinated artifact set. This boundary does not run mixed sets; roll back to the prior coordinated artifact set, or finish promoting the full set before launching.
notes: Illustrative seed boundary case; this is not a live release packet. This is the worked refuse-start example required by the acceptance criterion covering upgrade-order violation.
